title: Sprint 3 open the next chapter safely
summary: Sprint 3 should open one real next chapter using the current expedition and Route v2 model while landing another protective architectural split before the chapter broadens the codebase again.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the lane-1 next-chapter shell and protective split.
execution_order.why_now: The next chapter should not open without one more structural protection pass.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the lane-2 chapter science pack.
execution_order.why_now: A real next chapter needs its content pack queued before the shell opens.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the lane-3 expedition-grade chapter space.
execution_order.why_now: The chapter needs a physical place to chase, not just a teaser.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the lane-4 chapter Route v2 structure.
execution_order.why_now: The next chapter should reuse the current evidence and filing model rather than invent another shell.

guardrails: Open one real next chapter, not a broader dashboard.
Protect code health before expanding the expedition shell again.
Reuse the current calm traversal and evidence language.

main_307_focus.chapter_shell_target: Extend the existing single-card expedition shell so the logged Root Hollow state cleanly points at one real follow-on chapter through the current expedition-card model rather than another station page.
main_307_focus.protective_split_target: Extract the inline expedition-page body from src/engine/overlay-render.ts into a dedicated expedition-page helper, mirroring the earlier routes and nursery renderer splits.
non_goals: Do not add another field-station page.
Do not widen the routes-first station default.
Do not turn the expedition shell into a dashboard or multi-card board.

main_308_focus.chapter_target: one compact Treeline Pass science pack that strengthens the live High Pass chapter through current Stone Shelter and Low Fell carriers rather than opening a broader alpine wave
preferred_surfaces: focused runtime proof from live Treeline Pass inspectables
non_goals: Do not widen the atlas or journal shell.
Do not add another comparison-allowlist pass.
Do not reopen broader tundra content richness in this chunk.
Do not touch route-board or station copy.

main_309_focus.chapter_target: one compact Treeline Pass chapter pocket that makes the live High Pass opener feel like a real sheltered place, borrowing the forest family's remembered-middle pattern without reopening forest geometry
preferred_space: one shallow stone-break basin under the live lee shelf
optionally one tiny return step back into the upper shelf if the basin alone is too plain
non_goals: Do not reopen the right-side open-fell island or stack another lift to its right.
Do not move the tundra corridor door or travel logic.
Do not widen forest cave or giant-tree geometry in this chunk.
Do not add a new climbable, cue family, or text surface.

main_310_focus.chapter_target: one post-season High Pass Route v2 outing that replaces the filed-season placeholder direction copy with a real evidence-backed climb through the new Stone Shelter basin and out to the first exposed alpine hold
main_310_focus.support_target: spend the route-feel proof on one hand-lens bias for the crowded final talus-hold clue while keeping route-marker, note-tabs filing, and the existing support shell unchanged
main_310_focus.activation_guard: do not let the new request replace the calm season-close return beat while save.seasonCloseReturnPending is true
non_goals: Do not add another field-station page, planner row, or route board.
Do not reopen treeline geometry or a second alpine content wave.
Do not broaden the second season into multiple simultaneous routes.
Do not let the new route override the season-close return before the player reopens the station.
